title: buildah bud doesn't save ENV from base image
body:
**Description**
When building an image with buildah bud, the ENV from the base image is not saved in the new image built. This causes issues if some of the ENV is relevant when running the container image.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
Dockerfile:
```
FROM registry.fedoraproject.org/f26/redis
RUN touch /tmp/test.txt
```
1. buildah bud -t myimage [above dockerfile]
2. buildah inspect myimage

**Describe the results you received:**
With buildah bud, the ENV from the base image are missing. But when you build the same image with docker build, the ENV is available:

```
...
"Env": [
                "DISTTAG=f26container",
                "FGC=f26",
                "PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin",
                "NAME=redis",
                "VERSION=0",
                "RELEASE=6",
                "ARCH=x86_64",
                "REDIS_VERSION=0",
                "HOME=/var/lib/redis",
                "SUMMARY=Redis in-memory data structure store, used as database, cache and message broker",
                "DESCRIPTION=Redis 0 available as docker container, is an advanced key-value store. It is often referred to as a data structure server since keys can contain strings, hashes, lists, sets and sorted sets. You can run atomic operations on these types, like appending to a string; incrementing the value in a hash; pushing to a list; computing set intersection, union and difference; or getting the member with highest ranking in a sorted set. In order to achieve its outstanding performance, Redis works with an in-memory dataset. Depending on your use case, you can persist it either by dumping the dataset to disk every once in a while, or by appending each command to a log.",
                "CONTAINER_SCRIPTS_PATH=/usr/share/container-scripts/redis",
                "REDIS_PREFIX=/usr"
            ],
...
```


**Describe the results you expected:**
Expect the ENV to be in the new built image alsp.

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah-0.16-1.git82529cd.fc28.x86_64
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:       1.0
Go Version:    go1.10.1
Image Spec:    1.0.0
Runtime Spec:  1.0.0
Git Commit:    40325d3
Built:         Thu May 24 09:27:46 2018
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64

```

comments.body:
@rhatdan @nalind PTAL.
I can write up a fix, but just making sure  this wasn't intentional.
No this is definitely a bug.
